loginWithLink2ur.pageTitle: Login with Link²Ur - Third-Party Integration | Link²Ur
loginWithLink2ur.seoDescription: Add "Login with Link²Ur" to your website or app. OAuth 2.0 / OpenID Connect with authorization code and PKCE support.
loginWithLink2ur.title: Login with Link²Ur - Third-Party Integration
loginWithLink2ur.subtitle: Your site or app can offer "Login with Link²Ur" so users sign in with their Link²Ur account without registering again.
loginWithLink2ur.whatDesc: Link²Ur acts as an OAuth 2.0 / OpenID Connect provider. Your app can show a "Login with Link²Ur" button; users are sent to Link²Ur to sign in and consent, and your app receives a user identifier and optional profile (name, picture, email) without maintaining a separate registration.
loginWithLink2ur.whoDesc: Any website or app that wants "Login with Link²Ur" can integrate:
loginWithLink2ur.whoWeb: Web apps with a backend (confidential client; client_id and client_secret)
loginWithLink2ur.whoApp: SPAs or native apps (use PKCE; no client_secret in the client)
loginWithLink2ur.whoBackend: Backend or mobile using authorization code and optional refresh_token
loginWithLink2ur.howDesc: You need an OAuth client registered with Link²Ur (currently created by our admins for partners):
loginWithLink2ur.howStep1: Provide your app name and callback URL(s) (redirect_uri); we will whitelist them.
loginWithLink2ur.howStep2: Receive client_id and, for confidential clients, client_secret (keep it secure).
loginWithLink2ur.howStep3: Implement the OAuth 2.0 authorization code flow: redirect to our authorize page → user signs in and consents → your callback receives a code → exchange code for access_token and optionally call UserInfo.
loginWithLink2ur.techDesc: We expose a standard OIDC discovery endpoint so you can obtain authorization, token, and userinfo URLs and supported scopes and PKCE methods automatically.
loginWithLink2ur.contactDesc: To request a client or full integration docs (including request examples and error codes), email:

termsOfService.title: Terms of Service

serviceNature.title: 1. Nature of Service
serviceNature.content: Link²Ur is a local service platform for users in the UK, comprising: (1) Task Marketplace — connecting task posters with task takers; (2) Flea Market — for buying and selling secondhand items; (3) Community Forum — for discussions by university; (4) VIP Membership — optional auto-renewable subscription (in-app purchase via Apple App Store or other supported channels). We provide matching and listing services and process in-platform payments through Stripe (including card, Apple Pay and WeChat Pay where available). We may change, suspend or terminate access at any time.
serviceNature.testingPhase: Testing Phase Statement: The platform is currently in testing phase, features may be incomplete and services may be unstable. Users using this platform acknowledge and accept the risks that may exist during the testing phase.
serviceNature.paymentSystem: Payment System: The platform uses the Stripe payment system to process online payments. All payments are securely processed through Stripe, and the platform acts as an escrow service, transferring funds to task takers upon task completion.
serviceNature.recommendationSystem: Personalized Recommendation System: The platform provides personalized task recommendation services based on user behavior, preferences, and geographic location. The recommendation algorithm analyzes your browsing history, search records, task acceptance preferences, frequently visited locations, and other information to recommend tasks that better match your interests and needs. You can opt out of personalized recommendations through settings, but this may affect recommendation quality. Recommendation results are for reference only, and the platform does not assume responsibility for the accuracy or suitability of recommendation results.

userTypes.title: 2. User Types and Eligibility
userTypes.content: Only users aged 18 and above may register and use the service.
userTypes.userTypes: Users may be business users (B2B) or individual/consumer users (B2C). If you are a consumer, the Consumer Terms Appendix at the end applies.
userTypes.workEligibility: Work Eligibility Requirements: Before accepting tasks, users must ensure they have legal work eligibility. This includes but is not limited to: holding a valid work visa, possessing licenses or qualifications required for the relevant industry, and complying with local laws and regulations regarding the performance of related work. Users are responsible for confirming and complying with all applicable laws and regulations. The platform does not verify users' work eligibility, but reserves the right to require users to provide relevant proof.

platformPosition.title: 3. Platform Position and Prohibition of Off-Platform / Offline Transactions
platformPosition.content: We are a matching online platform, not an employer, contractor or agent for any tasks/projects. The platform only provides information matching services and does not participate in actual transaction execution.
platformPosition.offPlatform: Prohibition of off-platform and offline transactions: The platform prohibits users from conducting off-platform transactions and offline self-negotiation. Off-platform and offline transactions easily lead to disputes and carry fund and performance risks; the platform cannot provide guarantees or dispute resolution for them. Users must not induce or facilitate payment outside the platform, offline transactions, or bypassing platform dispute resolution (including but not limited to leaving contact details in chat to pay offline, directing the other party to pay outside the platform, etc.). Violations may result in content removal, restricted features or account suspension. If users nevertheless conduct off-platform or offline transactions, any contract, quality, delivery, refund, tax or dispute arising therefrom is solely between the parties; the platform assumes no responsibility or guarantee.
platformPosition.employmentStatus: Employment Relationship Statement: There is no employment relationship between the platform and users. Users provide services as self-employed/independent contractors and bear full responsibility for their services, including but not limited to service quality, tax reporting, insurance, etc. The platform is not responsible for users' service quality, safety or any losses.
platformPosition.offlineTransactions: Prohibition of offline transactions: The platform prohibits offline self-negotiation and offline transactions. If users nevertheless conduct offline transactions, the platform assumes no responsibility; users should assess and bear transaction risks; the platform bears no responsibility for transaction outcomes, service quality, fund safety, etc.
platformPosition.scopeOfServices:
Scope of services (please note):
• Task Marketplace: Payment and dispute resolution must be completed through the platform; off-platform and offline transactions are prohibited. The guaranteed payment (escrow), refund policy and dispute/arbitration rules in these terms apply only to task payments completed through the platform.
• Flea Market: The platform only provides listing and matching; off-platform and offline transactions are prohibited; users must communicate on the platform and must not induce the other party to pay offline or transact privately. The platform does not provide payment or escrow; if users nevertheless conduct off-platform/offline transactions, the platform is not responsible for outcomes, fund safety or product quality.
• Community Forum: Discussion areas by university/school; the platform only provides posting and exchange of information; users are responsible for their posts; the platform does not mediate disputes between users (unless required by law).
• VIP Membership: Optional auto-renewable subscription purchased via Apple App Store or other channels; each channel’s terms and refund policy apply; VIP benefits (e.g. task listing priority) are as displayed on the platform.

feesAndRules.title: 4. Fees and Platform Rules
feesAndRules.content: Platform Service Fee: The platform charges a percentage service fee from each task payment (specific rates are displayed on the platform). The service fee is automatically deducted at payment, and the remaining amount is held in escrow and transferred to the task taker upon task completion.
feesAndRules.reviews: Reviews and Content: Prohibited are false/misleading reviews, review manipulation or organizing "positive review tasks". We may delete related content and restrict or terminate accounts.
feesAndRules.paymentProcessing: Payment Processing: All payments are processed through the Stripe payment system. The platform acts as an escrow service, holding funds in the platform account after the task poster pays, and transferring them to the task taker upon task completion.
feesAndRules.applicationFee:
Platform Service Fee: The platform deducts a service fee from the task amount. The service fee calculation rules are as follows:
• Micro Tasks (Task Amount < £10): A fixed fee of £1 is charged as the Micro Task Service Fee
• Regular Tasks (Task Amount ≥ £10): A service fee of 10% of the task amount is charged
The service fee includes but is not limited to: payment transfer fees, platform operating costs (servers, bandwidth, system maintenance, etc.), human resource costs (customer support, technical development, operations management, etc.), risk guarantee funds, and other reasonable costs required for platform operations. The service fee is used to maintain platform operations, provide technical support and service guarantees. Specific rates are displayed on the platform.

pointsRules.title: 4.1 Points Rules
pointsRules.intro: Platform points are virtual benefit units issued by the platform to users and may only be used within the scope of platform rules.
pointsRules.earn: How to earn: Users may earn points by completing tasks, inviting new users, participating in activities, etc. Specific rules and amounts are as published or stated in the relevant activity.
pointsRules.use: How to use: Points may be used to redeem designated coupons or benefits; they cannot be used directly as cash or withdrawn. Each redemption is subject to the platform's current rules.
pointsRules.expire: Validity and expiry: Points may be subject to a validity period or expiry rules; if the platform has enabled point expiry, unused points after expiry will be cleared. Specific validity is as published.
pointsRules.value: Points are not currency: Points have no monetary value, are non-transferable, cannot be exchanged for cash or legal property, and may only be used within the account holder's account.
pointsRules.adjust: Rule changes: The platform may adjust point earning rates, use scope, validity and other rules as needed; changes will be communicated by notice or publication; material changes will be notified with reasonable advance notice.

couponRules.title: 4.2 Coupon Rules
couponRules.intro: Coupons are discount vouchers issued by the platform or partners and may only be used in designated platform scenarios.
couponRules.claim: Claiming and eligibility: Users may obtain coupons via redemption codes, activity pages, or point redemption. Eligibility, per-user limits and conditions are as stated on the coupon or published by the platform.
couponRules.use: Conditions of use: Coupons are typically subject to minimum spend, applicable services (e.g. task payment only) and validity; use must comply with both the coupon and platform rules.
couponRules.type: Type and value: Coupons may offer a fixed discount or percentage discount as stated on the coupon or detail page. Rules for use per order (e.g. stacking) are as published at the time.
couponRules.refund: Refunds and invalidity: If an order paid with a coupon is refunded, platform refund rules apply; used coupons may not be returned or may be voided. Unused coupons expire automatically after the validity period.
couponRules.prohibit: Prohibited conduct: Bulk acquisition, resale or counterfeiting of coupons by improper means is prohibited; violations may result in coupon voiding and account action.
couponRules.adjust: Rule changes: The platform may adjust coupon issuance, use rules and scope; changes take effect as published.

paymentAndRefund.title: 5. Payment and Refund
paymentAndRefund.paymentMethod: Payment Methods: The platform supports online payments through the Stripe payment system, including credit cards, debit cards and other payment methods.
paymentAndRefund.paymentProcess: Payment Process: After accepting an application, task posters need to complete payment through the Stripe payment system. Once payment is successful, funds will be held in the platform account.
paymentAndRefund.escrowService: Escrow Service: The platform provides escrow services to ensure the security of task poster funds. Funds will be transferred to the task taker upon task completion.
paymentAndRefund.completionConfirmation: Task Completion Confirmation: After a task is completed, the task poster needs to confirm task completion. Once confirmed, funds will be transferred from the escrow account to the task taker. The completion confirmation is an important milestone that affects the applicability of the refund policy.
paymentAndRefund.refundBeforeConfirmation: Refund Policy Before Completion Confirmation: Before the task poster confirms task completion, the task poster may apply for a refund. Refund applications require evidence from both parties, including but not limited to: (1) Task status screenshots; (2) Communication records between both parties (in-platform messages, emails, etc.); (3) Comparison between task description and actual delivery results; (4) Time proof (such as comparison between task deadline and actual completion time); (5) Other relevant evidence. The platform will review within 3-5 business days after receiving the application, and may contact both parties if necessary. If both parties cannot reach an agreement, the platform will make a decision based on factors such as task completion status, communication records, evidence materials, etc.
paymentAndRefund.appealAfterConfirmation: Appeal Policy After Completion Confirmation: After the task poster confirms task completion, refund applications will generally not be accepted. If the task poster believes there are serious issues (such as fraud, breach of contract, or other serious misconduct by the task taker), they may file an appeal with the platform. Appeals require sufficient evidence proving serious violations by the task taker. The platform will review based on the appeal content and evidence, but does not guarantee that funds can be recovered. The platform reserves the right to decide whether to process appeals based on specific circumstances and assumes no guarantee or responsibility for recovering funds.
paymentAndRefund.refundEligibility: Refund Eligibility: Task posters are eligible to apply for refunds only under the following circumstances:
paymentAndRefund.refundConditions:
(1) Task Not Started: Task status is pending_payment (pending payment) or in_progress (in progress) but the task taker has not actually started work.
(2) Task Not Completed and Not Accepted: The task taker failed to complete the task as agreed, and the task poster has not confirmed task completion (task status is pending_confirmation or in_progress).
(3) Task Quality Does Not Meet Requirements: The task is completed but the quality clearly does not meet the task description or agreement, and the task poster can provide sufficient evidence.
(4) Task Taker Breach of Contract: The task taker violated the agreement, such as failing to complete on time, abandoning midway, providing false services, etc.
paymentAndRefund.refundEvidenceList:
• Task Status Screenshot: Showing current task status (pending_payment, in_progress, pending_confirmation, etc.)
• Communication Records: In-platform messages, emails or other communication records with the task taker, proving the task has not started, not completed or quality does not meet requirements
• Task Description Comparison: If applying for refund due to quality issues, provide comparison evidence between task description and actual delivery results
• Time Proof: If applying for refund due to late completion, provide comparison between task deadline and actual completion time
• Other Relevant Evidence: Depending on the specific situation, the platform may require other relevant evidence
paymentAndRefund.refundProcessSteps:
(1) Submit Application: Task poster submits refund application through the platform and uploads relevant evidence.
(2) Platform Review: The platform will review within 3-5 business days after receiving the application, and may contact both parties if necessary to understand the situation.
(3) Mutual Negotiation: If the task has started, the platform will attempt to facilitate mutual negotiation. If both parties reach an agreement, process according to the negotiation results.
(4) Platform Decision: If both parties cannot reach an agreement, the platform will make a decision based on factors such as task completion status, communication records, evidence materials, etc.
(5) Refund Processing: If the refund application is approved, the platform will refund through the original payment method within 5-10 business days (deducting platform service fees already incurred).
paymentAndRefund.refundTimeLimitDetails:
• Before Task Starts: Can apply for refund at any time before the task starts
• During Task: Can apply for refund when task status is in_progress or pending_confirmation
• Before Completion Confirmation: Before the task poster confirms task completion, can apply for refund, requires evidence from both parties
• After Completion Confirmation: After the task poster confirms task completion, refund applications will generally not be accepted, can only file an appeal, but no guarantee of fund recovery
paymentAndRefund.nonRefundableList:
• Task is completed and task poster has confirmed completion (is_confirmed = 1)
• Task poster changed their mind for personal reasons (such as no longer needing the service)
• Task poster failed to provide sufficient refund evidence
• Task taker has completed work as agreed, but task poster is not satisfied (unless there is evidence that quality clearly does not meet requirements)
• Task cannot be completed due to force majeure (such as natural disasters, government policy changes, etc.)
paymentAndRefund.refundWarning: Important Notice: Task completion confirmation is an irreversible step. Once confirmed, funds will be transferred to the task taker, and refunds will become very difficult. Please carefully check task completion before confirming to ensure the task meets your requirements.
paymentAndRefund.partialRefundDetails:
In some cases, the platform may approve partial refunds:
• Partial Task Completion: If the task taker has completed part of the work, the platform may approve a partial refund based on the value of the completed portion
• Partial Quality Issues: If task quality partially does not meet requirements but still has some value, the platform may approve a partial refund
• Mutual Agreement: If both parties agree on a partial refund, the platform will process according to the agreement
paymentAndRefund.refundDisputeDetails:
If there is a dispute regarding refunds:
(1) The platform will first attempt to facilitate mutual negotiation
(2) If negotiation fails, the platform will make a decision based on the following factors:
  • Task completion status (whether completed, completion quality)
  • Communication records between both parties (whether there is clear agreement, whether there are disputes)
  • Evidence materials (whether sufficient, whether credible)
  • Platform rules and fairness principles
(3) Platform decision is final, both parties must comply
(4) If there is objection to the platform decision, can be resolved through legal channels
paymentAndRefund.refundProcessingDetails:
• All refunds will be processed through the original payment method (if original payment method is unavailable, platform will contact user to provide alternative refund method)
• Refund processing time is typically 5-10 business days (from the date of refund application approval)
• Platform service fees will be deducted from the refund amount (if task is partially completed, service fees are deducted proportionally)
• After successful refund, platform will notify user via email or in-platform message
paymentAndRefund.chargebackDetails:
If a chargeback occurs:
• The platform will cooperate with the payment processor (Stripe) to investigate
• The platform will provide relevant transaction records, communication records and other evidence
• Based on investigation results, the platform will take appropriate measures
• If the chargeback is determined to be malicious, the platform reserves the right to pursue legal action
paymentAndRefund.paymentSecurity: Payment Security: The platform uses the Stripe payment system, which complies with PCI DSS security standards to ensure payment information security. The platform does not store your complete payment credentials.
paymentAndRefund.guaranteePaymentIntro: The platform provides guaranteed payment (escrow) for task payments completed through the platform: funds paid by the task poster are held by the platform until the task is completed or a dispute is resolved, and are not paid directly to the task taker, so as to reduce transaction risk.
paymentAndRefund.guaranteeWhat:
What the platform guarantees:
• Secure holding of funds: After payment and until release, funds are held by the platform through compliant payment channels (e.g. Stripe) and are not released to either party in advance
• Release according to rules: Funds are released or refunded only when (1) the task poster confirms task completion, or (2) the platform has made a decision under the dispute resolution rules and the decision is implemented
• Payment data security: Processing through Stripe and other PCI DSS-compliant processors; the platform does not store full payment credentials
paymentAndRefund.guaranteeWhatNot:
What the platform does not guarantee:
• Task quality, delivery or outcome: The platform does not guarantee the quality, timeliness or compliance of services provided by the task taker
• Off-platform/offline transactions: Off-platform and offline transactions are prohibited; the platform does not guarantee and is not responsible for them (see "Platform Position and Prohibition of Off-Platform / Offline Transactions")
• Delays in payment or receipt due to force majeure or third-party causes: The platform will assist where reasonable but will not be liable beyond the liability cap in these terms
paymentAndRefund.fundReleaseConditions:
When funds are released:
• Normal completion: When the task poster confirms completion in the platform, escrowed funds (after platform fee) are transferred to the task taker as agreed
• Refund / partial refund: Before confirmation, the task poster may apply for a refund; if a dispute arises, the platform will mediate or decide, and funds will be refunded or partially refunded per that decision, with the remainder released to the task taker
• After a dispute decision: The platform’s dispute decision (including who receives funds and any partial refund) is final for platform purposes; both parties must comply with the release or refund as decided

prohibitedTasks.title: 6. Prohibited Task Types
prohibitedTasks.introduction: To maintain platform safety, legality and user experience, the following types of tasks are strictly prohibited on the platform:
prohibitedTasks.illegalActivities: (1) Illegal Activities: Any tasks that violate UK laws and regulations or international law, including but not limited to:
prohibitedTasks.illegalActivitiesList:
• Drug trafficking, illegal drug-related services
• Weapons, firearms, explosives trading or related services
• Human trafficking, sex trade, prostitution services
• Money laundering, financial fraud, illegal fundraising
• Counterfeit documents, files, currency
• Hacking services, cyber attacks, malicious software development
• Services that infringe on others' intellectual property (such as pirated software, pirated content)
prohibitedTasks.harmfulContent: (2) Harmful Content: Tasks that may cause harm to users or society, including but not limited to:
prohibitedTasks.harmfulContentList:
• Violence, threats, intimidation services
• Harassment, stalking, malicious defamation services
• Spreading hate speech, discriminatory content
• Inappropriate content involving minors
• Animal abuse-related services
prohibitedTasks.fraudulentServices: (3) Fraudulent Services: False, misleading or fraudulent tasks, including but not limited to:
prohibitedTasks.fraudulentServicesList:
• Fake reviews, fake orders, review manipulation services
• Fake identity verification, fake certification services
• Exam cheating, exam proxy, essay writing services (academic dishonesty)
• Fake investments, pyramid schemes, Ponzi schemes
• Fake prizes, fake discount information
prohibitedTasks.regulatedServices: (4) Regulated Services Without Qualifications: Tasks that require specific licenses, qualifications or certifications but the poster or taker does not have the required qualifications, including but not limited to:
prohibitedTasks.regulatedServicesList:
• Medical services, medical consultation (requires medical practice license)
• Legal services, legal consultation (requires lawyer practice license)
• Financial services, investment advice (requires financial regulatory license)
• Construction, renovation services (requires construction qualifications)
• Food processing, catering services (requires food safety license)
• Educational services, training (requires educational institution qualifications)
• Driving instruction (requires driving instructor qualification)
prohibitedTasks.platformAbuse: (5) Platform Abuse: Tasks that misuse the platform, including but not limited to:
prohibitedTasks.platformAbuseList:
• Fake orders, fake reviews, fake transactions
• Cash-out, money laundering, fund transfers
• Malicious competition, malicious reporting
• System vulnerability exploitation, automated script abuse
prohibitedTasks.otherProhibitedList:
• Trading of personal privacy information
• Trading of others' accounts, passwords, personal information
• Gambling, betting-related services
• Politically sensitive content
• Other content that violates platform rules or public order and morals
prohibitedTasks.enforcement: Enforcement: If prohibited task types are found, the platform will immediately delete the task and take measures against the poster including warnings, function restrictions, account suspension or permanent ban based on the severity of the violation. The platform reserves the right to report to relevant regulatory or law enforcement agencies.

userBehavior.title: 7. User Behavior and Prohibited Activities
userBehavior.prohibited: In addition to prohibited task types, users may not: (1) publish illegal, infringing, fraudulent, misleading or harmful content; (2) publish tasks requiring licenses without proper qualifications; (3) scrape, attack, bypass access controls or attempt to damage the system; (4) engage in money laundering, sanctions evasion or other financial crimes; (5) malicious fake orders, fake transactions, cash-out, etc.; (6) induce or facilitate off-platform transactions, offline self-negotiation, payment outside the platform or bypassing platform dispute resolution.
userBehavior.consequences: If violated, we may delete content, restrict/terminate access, and report to regulatory or law enforcement agencies when necessary.
userBehavior.accountSuspension: Account Suspension Policy: If the platform discovers or becomes aware of any illegal or violations by users, including but not limited to violations of these terms, engaging in illegal activities, fraud, harassment of other users, publishing false information, etc., the platform has the right and obligation to immediately suspend the user's account. Account suspension may include but is not limited to: temporary account suspension, permanent account ban, restriction of account functions, deletion of violating content, etc. The platform will take appropriate measures based on the severity of the violations and reserves the right to report to relevant regulatory or law enforcement agencies. After a user's account is suspended, they will not be able to continue using the platform services, published tasks may be deleted, and ongoing transactions may be terminated.

userResponsibilities.title: 8. User Responsibilities and Obligations
userResponsibilities.introduction: By using the platform services, users undertake the following responsibilities and obligations:
userResponsibilities.accountSecurityList:
• Users are responsible for safeguarding account information (username, password, email, etc.) and must not disclose it to others
• Users are responsible for ensuring account security; if the account is compromised or abnormal, they must notify the platform and take appropriate measures promptly
• Users are responsible for all operations under their account, including but not limited to posting tasks, accepting tasks, making payments, receiving payments
• Users must not transfer, sell or rent their account to others
userResponsibilities.accurateInformationList:
• Users are responsible for providing true, accurate and complete personal information
• Users are responsible for ensuring that task information posted is true, accurate and complete, and must not post false or misleading information
• Users are responsible for clearly stating in the task description the task requirements, deadline, remuneration and other key information
• Users are responsible for updating personal information in a timely manner to ensure it remains current
userResponsibilities.legalComplianceList:
• Users are responsible for complying with all applicable laws and regulations, including but not limited to UK law and international law
• Users are responsible for ensuring that posted tasks do not violate laws or infringe others' rights
• Users are responsible for ensuring they have the legal capacity to perform the task (e.g. work visa, relevant licences)
• Users are responsible for complying with the platform's rules and policies
userResponsibilities.taskCompletionList:
• Task poster responsibilities:
  - Provide clear and accurate task descriptions and requirements
  - Respond promptly to task taker enquiries and communication
  - Confirm task completion in a timely manner after the task is completed
  - Pay task remuneration as agreed
• Task taker responsibilities:
  - Read the task description carefully and ensure understanding of the requirements
  - Complete the work in accordance with the task description and requirements
  - Complete the task on time (unless otherwise agreed)
  - Ensure work quality meets the task requirements
  - Communicate task progress to the task poster in a timely manner
userResponsibilities.paymentResponsibilityList:
• Task poster responsibilities:
  - Complete payment promptly after accepting an application
  - Ensure the payment account has sufficient balance
  - Must not maliciously refuse payment or make false payments
  - Cooperate with the platform in handling payment-related issues
• Task taker responsibilities:
  - Provide accurate payment receipt information (e.g. Stripe Connect account)
  - Ensure the receipt account is in normal use
  - Cooperate with the platform in handling receipt-related issues
userResponsibilities.communicationResponsibilityList:
• Users are responsible for maintaining a polite and professional attitude in communication
• Users are responsible for replying to the other party's enquiries and messages in a timely manner
• Users are responsible for communicating task progress in a timely manner during the task
• Users are responsible for communicating promptly when issues arise and seeking solutions
userResponsibilities.disputeResolutionList:
• Users are responsible for first attempting to resolve disputes with the other party through good-faith negotiation
• Users are responsible for cooperating with the platform's dispute mediation
• Users are responsible for providing true and sufficient evidence
• Users are responsible for complying with the platform's dispute resolution decisions
userResponsibilities.contentResponsibilityList:
• Users are responsible for ensuring that posted content does not infringe others' intellectual property (e.g. copyright, trademark, patent)
• Users are responsible for ensuring that posted content does not infringe others' privacy, portrait rights, etc.
• Users are responsible for ensuring that posted content does not contain illegal, harmful or inappropriate information
• Users bear full legal responsibility for the content they post
userResponsibilities.taxResponsibilityList:
• Users are responsible for declaring and paying relevant taxes (e.g. income tax, VAT) themselves
• Users are responsible for retaining relevant transaction records for tax authority review
• The platform does not withhold or pay user taxes and does not provide tax advice
userResponsibilities.indemnificationDetails:
Users agree to indemnify and hold harmless the platform, its affiliates, employees and agents from any claims, losses, liabilities, costs and expenses (including reasonable legal fees) arising from:
• User's breach of these terms
• User's breach of any laws or regulations
• User's infringement of others' rights (e.g. intellectual property, privacy)
• Any loss caused by content posted or services provided by the user
userResponsibilities.consequences: Consequences of Breach of Responsibilities and Obligations:
userResponsibilities.consequencesDetails:
If a user breaches the above responsibilities and obligations:
• The platform has the right to delete the relevant content immediately
• The platform has the right to restrict or terminate the user's account
• The platform has the right to refuse to process the relevant transaction or refund application
• The platform has the right to report to relevant regulatory or law enforcement agencies
• The user may bear corresponding legal liability and financial loss

intellectualProperty.title: 9. Intellectual Property and User Content
intellectualProperty.platformRights: All rights to the platform and its content belong to us or our licensors.
intellectualProperty.userContent: You retain rights to uploaded content, but for the purpose of providing, operating and promoting the service, you grant us a worldwide, non-exclusive, royalty-free, sublicensable license (for display, backup, review, indexing and promotion).
intellectualProperty.complaints: Infringement complaints are handled through our notice-and-takedown process (please contact us via the above email).

privacyData.title: 10. Privacy and Data (UK GDPR)
privacyData.controller: We act as a **data controller** processing account, log and communication data, only within the scope of "providing services, ensuring security, improving experience", as detailed in the Privacy Notice below; you may exercise rights of access, rectification, erasure, restriction of processing, data portability, objection to processing, withdrawal of consent, etc.; you may also complain to the ICO.
privacyData.payments: Payment Processing: The platform uses the Stripe payment system to process payments. Stripe, as the payment processor, collects and processes necessary payment information. We only receive payment status and necessary reconciliation information, and do not access complete payment credentials (such as full credit card numbers). All payment data security is handled by Stripe in compliance with PCI DSS standards.

disclaimer.title: 11. Disclaimer and Limitation of Liability
disclaimer.service: The service is provided "as is" / "as available" without warranty of uninterrupted or error-free operation.
disclaimer.liability: We are not liable for indirect, consequential, punitive damages or loss of profits/goodwill.
disclaimer.limit: Total liability cap: For any single or combined claim, our liability is limited to the total amount you actually paid us in the past 12 months or **£100 (whichever is higher)**; except for legally mandatory liability (such as personal injury or death due to negligence).

termination.title: 12. Termination and Data Retention
termination.content: You or we may terminate test access at any time. To comply with legal obligations and legitimate purposes, we may retain logs and records for the shortest necessary period (as detailed in the Privacy Notice (Beta)).
termination.effect: Termination does not affect existing rights and obligations.

disputes.title: 13. Disputes and Applicable Law
disputes.negotiation: Disputes between users arising from tasks, payment or services should first be resolved by good-faith negotiation; if that fails, the parties may use the dispute and arbitration rules below via the platform; if still unresolved, they may submit to the courts of England and Wales.
disputes.law: These terms are governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of England and Wales.
disputes.whoCanSubmit: Who may raise a dispute: Either party to a task or order (task poster or task taker) may raise a dispute on the platform if they believe the other party has not performed as agreed, has breached the agreement, or if there is disagreement about completion or who should receive funds.
disputes.howToSubmit: How and when to submit: Use the "Submit Dispute" option on the relevant task or order page, state the reason and upload evidence where possible (e.g. messages, task description vs delivery, time proof). It is advisable to submit while the task is in progress or pending confirmation so the platform can intervene in time; appeals after confirmation are subject to the appeal policy under "Payment and Refund".
disputes.platformMediation: Platform mediation: After a dispute is submitted, the platform will first try to help the parties reach an agreement (e.g. via in-app messages or email). If they agree within a reasonable time set by the platform (e.g. on refund amount or additional delivery), funds and order status will be handled according to that agreement.
disputes.platformDecision: Platform decision: If the parties do not agree or one party does not cooperate within a reasonable time, the platform will decide based on the evidence and rules. It will consider: task description and agreement, communication between the parties, completion and evidence, these terms and platform rules, and fairness. The platform may ask both parties to provide further evidence or explanation.
disputes.decisionFactors: Factors considered include but are not limited to: whether the task was completed as agreed, whether quality clearly fell short of the agreement, whether there was delay or unauthorised change, whether there was a clear written agreement (including in-app messages), and whether evidence is sufficient and credible. The platform does not guarantee to uphold either party’s position; the decision is based on evidence and rules.
disputes.decisionBinding: Effect of the decision: The platform’s decision on that dispute (e.g. dismiss dispute, full or partial refund, release all or part of funds to the task taker) is final for platform purposes; both parties must comply and cooperate (e.g. confirm refund, allow release). The platform will implement the release or refund according to the decision.
disputes.legalRemedy: Legal remedy: If a party disagrees with the platform’s decision, they may still seek a remedy from a court of competent jurisdiction (England and Wales) under applicable law; however, any release or refund already made by the platform will not be reversed automatically because of later litigation unless required by a final court order.

forumTerms.title: Forum Service Terms
forumTerms.intro: The platform provides community forum functionality by university/school, allowing users to post, reply, like and favourite. The forum aims to facilitate information exchange and experience sharing.
forumTerms.contentResponsibility: Content responsibility: Users are fully responsible for all content they post (text, images, links, etc.) and must ensure it does not violate laws, infringe others’ IP or privacy, or contain defamation, harassment, hate speech, discrimination, false information or spam.
forumTerms.moderation: Moderation and removal: We reserve the right to review, edit, delete or refuse to publish any forum content without prior notice. Content may be removed for violations, infringement, inappropriate content or community standards.
forumTerms.conduct: Users must not spam, attack or harass others, impersonate others, post content that endangers safety, or use the forum for illegal activity or to compromise the system. Violations may result in content removal or account restrictions.
forumTerms.disclaimer: Forum disclaimer: The platform is not responsible for user-generated content in the forum. Disputes between users are resolved by users themselves; the platform does not mediate (unless required by law) and is not liable for direct or indirect loss from use or inability to use the forum.

fleaMarketTerms.title: Flea Market Service Terms
fleaMarketTerms.intro: The platform provides flea market functionality, allowing users to list, browse, buy and sell second-hand items. The flea market aims to facilitate second-hand transactions between users.
fleaMarketTerms.platformPosition: Platform position: The platform only provides listing and matching and does not participate in actual transactions. Off-platform and offline transactions are prohibited; users must communicate on the platform and must not induce the other party to pay offline or transact privately. The platform does not provide payment, logistics or escrow; if users nevertheless conduct off-platform/offline transactions, the platform is not responsible for transaction outcomes, product quality or fund safety.
fleaMarketTerms.sellerResponsibility: Sellers must ensure product information is true, accurate and complete; must not publish false information or conceal material defects. Trading in items prohibited by law, counterfeits, infringing or dangerous goods is prohibited (see platform rules).
fleaMarketTerms.riskNotice: Transaction risk: All transactions are completed by buyers and sellers. The platform is not responsible for product quality, authenticity, delivery, logistics or transaction disputes. We recommend full communication, in-person exchange where possible and keeping records; for high-value items consider secure payment methods.
fleaMarketTerms.disclaimer: Flea market disclaimer: The platform is not responsible for any transactions in the flea market. Disputes or losses arising from transactions are resolved by the parties. The platform is not liable for any direct or indirect loss from use or inability to use the flea market.

consumerAppendix.title: Consumer Terms Appendix (Applicable when user is a consumer)
consumerAppendix.freeService: When you use our free matching service as a consumer, we will provide key information on the interface (platform identity, contact information, main functions and any important limitations).
consumerAppendix.futureCharges: Currently during the testing period we do not charge consumers, so the cooling-off/refund mechanism for paid digital services is not triggered; once we charge consumers in the future, we will comply with relevant consumer regulations (including information disclosure, cancellation rights and prohibition of hidden fees/false reviews, etc.) and notify prominently before charging.

expertTerms.title: Task Expert Service Terms
expertTerms.intro: Task Experts are outstanding users certified by the Link²Ur platform who possess professional skills and extensive experience in specific fields, capable of providing high-quality and reliable services to task posters. These terms apply to all users who have obtained or are applying for Task Expert qualification and should be read alongside other terms of this Agreement.
expertTerms.certificationRequirements: Certification Requirements and Process: (1) Eligibility: Users must meet the basic conditions published by the platform (such as completing a certain number of tasks, receiving good reviews, etc.) before they can apply for Task Expert certification. (2) Application Materials: Users must submit an application on their profile page and provide relevant supporting materials, including but not limited to: skill certificates, portfolios, work experience proof, educational credentials, etc. (3) Platform Review: The platform will review application materials, with a review period typically of 5-10 working days. The platform may request additional materials or conduct interviews. (4) Certification Result: After passing the review, users will receive the Task Expert certification badge and enjoy expert-exclusive privileges. The platform reserves the right to reject applications that do not meet the criteria without providing specific reasons.
expertTerms.expertLevels: Expert Levels: The platform offers multiple expert levels (such as Regular Expert, VIP Expert, Super Expert, etc.). Specific level classification criteria and benefits are subject to platform announcements. Levels may be dynamically adjusted based on factors such as service performance, reviews, and task completion volume.
expertTerms.obligations: Expert Obligations: (1) Service Quality Assurance: Experts shall ensure that their services meet the quality standards published by the platform, completing accepted tasks on time and to standard. (2) Information Accuracy: Experts must ensure that their certification materials and profile information are true, accurate, and complete; they must not forge or exaggerate qualifications. (3) Professional Conduct: Experts shall maintain a professional attitude during service, comply with industry standards and professional ethics, and must not engage in fraud, misleading or detrimental conduct. (4) Response Timeliness: Experts shall respond to task posters' enquiries and communications within the timeframes specified by the platform, and provide timely task progress updates. (5) Compliance: Experts must comply with all applicable laws and regulations to ensure their services are lawful and compliant. (6) Confidentiality: Experts have a duty of confidentiality regarding user information and business secrets obtained during the course of service, and must not disclose or use such information for other purposes.
expertTerms.serviceStandards: Service Standards: (1) Experts shall complete tasks according to the task description and standards agreed by both parties; any deviations should be promptly communicated and negotiated with the task poster. (2) Experts must not promote products or services unrelated to the task during the service process. (3) Experts must not use their expert status to guide users towards off-platform transactions or offline self-negotiation. (4) Experts shall actively cooperate with the platform's dispute resolution and refund review processes.
expertTerms.feesAndCommission: Fees and Commission: The service fee rates applicable to experts may differ from those for regular users; specific rates are subject to platform announcements. The platform may set differentiated service fee rates based on factors such as expert level and task type. Income obtained by experts through the platform is subject to their own tax responsibilities (see Section 8 'User Responsibilities and Obligations' — 'Tax Responsibility' of this Agreement).
expertTerms.multiPersonTasks: Multi-Person Task Rules: (1) Experts may publish multi-person tasks (activities) through the platform, recruiting multiple participants to complete together. (2) As the publisher of multi-person tasks, experts bear primary responsibility for task organisation, coordination, and quality. (3) Payment, refund, and dispute resolution for multi-person tasks are subject to Section 5 'Payment and Refund' of this Agreement. (4) Experts shall clearly specify key information when publishing multi-person tasks, including task requirements, time, location, participant limits, cost allocation, etc. (5) Participants who withdraw from multi-person tasks for any reason shall be handled according to the platform's published withdrawal rules.
expertTerms.revocation: Qualification Revocation and Demotion: The platform reserves the right to revoke or downgrade expert qualification in any of the following circumstances: (1) Providing false certification materials or information. (2) Persistently substandard service quality, with multiple negative reviews or complaints. (3) Violating this Agreement or platform rules. (4) Receiving serious user complaints or involvement in fraud or illegal activities. (5) Prolonged inactivity (specific criteria subject to platform announcements). After revocation, the expert's certification badge and exclusive privileges will be cancelled; tasks in progress will not be affected, but no new tasks may be accepted. Experts may reapply for certification after meeting the conditions.
expertTerms.disclaimer: Expert Disclaimer: Platform certification of experts only indicates that the user met the basic conditions set by the platform at the time of application, and does not constitute a guarantee or endorsement of the expert's service quality, professional capability, or reliability. Users should assess risks independently when choosing expert services. The platform is not liable for service outcomes, quality, or any losses caused by expert services.

aiServiceTerms.title: AI Assistant Service Terms
aiServiceTerms.intro: The Link²Ur platform provides the AI assistant 'Linker' service (hereinafter 'AI Assistant'), which helps users check task status, search tasks, learn about platform rules, view points and coupons, and assist with task posting. By using the AI Assistant, you agree to these terms.
aiServiceTerms.natureOfService: Nature of Service: The AI Assistant is based on large language model artificial intelligence technology, providing automated information enquiry and assistance services. The AI Assistant is an auxiliary tool only and does not replace human customer service or professional advice. The AI Assistant's responses are based on its training data and current platform information, and may be inaccurate, incomplete, or outdated.
aiServiceTerms.accuracyDisclaimer: Accuracy Disclaimer: Content generated by the AI Assistant is for reference only and does not constitute legal, financial, medical, or other professional advice. The platform does not guarantee the accuracy, completeness, or timeliness of AI Assistant responses. Users should not rely solely on AI Assistant responses for important decisions. If information provided by the AI Assistant conflicts with official platform announcements or these terms, the official platform announcements and these terms shall prevail.
aiServiceTerms.recommendationDisclaimer: Recommendation Disclaimer: The AI Assistant may provide task recommendations or suggestions based on user queries. Such recommendations do not constitute platform endorsement or guarantee of specific tasks, users, or transactions. Users should independently assess the suitability and risks of recommended content.
aiServiceTerms.taskAssistance: Task Posting Assistance: The AI Assistant may help users draft task posting content. Users are responsible for reviewing and confirming AI-generated task content before posting, ensuring it is true, accurate, and lawful. The platform is not liable for issues arising from users failing to review AI-generated content.
aiServiceTerms.dataHandling: Data Handling: User conversation data processed by the AI Assistant is used to provide and improve services. The AI Assistant automatically analyses your conversations to extract interest preferences, skills, life stages, and service preferences to generate user profile data. This profile data is used to optimise AI response quality and the platform's task recommendation algorithm. User profile data is stored in aggregated form and does not contain original conversation text. You can opt out of AI-analysis-based personalised recommendations in settings. The platform will not use AI conversation or profile data for purposes unrelated to the service. Storage and protection of AI conversation data is subject to the relevant provisions of this platform's Privacy Notice. See the 'AI Assistant Data' section in the Privacy Notice below.
aiServiceTerms.limitations: Usage Limitations: Users must not use the AI Assistant to generate illegal, harmful, fraudulent, or content that violates platform rules. Users must not attempt to bypass the AI Assistant's safety restrictions through special prompts or techniques. The platform reserves the right to adjust, suspend, or terminate the AI Assistant service without prior notice.
aiServiceTerms.liability: Limitation of Liability: The platform is not liable for any direct or indirect losses arising from use of or inability to use the AI Assistant service, including but not limited to: decision errors caused by inaccurate AI responses, inconvenience caused by AI service interruptions, or transaction disputes caused by AI recommendations.

vipSubscriptionTerms.title: VIP Membership Subscription Terms
vipSubscriptionTerms.intro: Link²Ur offers a VIP membership subscription service (hereinafter 'VIP Subscription'), providing exclusive benefits and value-added services to subscribing users. These terms apply to all users who purchase or use VIP Subscription.
vipSubscriptionTerms.subscriptionMethod: Subscription Method: VIP Subscription is purchased through Apple App Store In-App Purchase. The subscription transaction is processed by Apple and is subject to Apple's payment terms and conditions. The platform does not directly process VIP Subscription payments.
vipSubscriptionTerms.autoRenewal: Auto-Renewal: VIP Subscription is an auto-renewable subscription. The subscription will automatically renew and charge at the end of each subscription period, unless you cancel at least 24 hours before the end of the current subscription period. Auto-renewal fees will be charged at the price at which you subscribed, unless the platform provides advance notice of a price change.
vipSubscriptionTerms.cancellation: Cancellation: You may cancel your VIP Subscription at any time via the following methods: (1) On iPhone/iPad: Settings → Apple ID → Subscriptions → Find Link²Ur VIP → Cancel Subscription. (2) After cancellation, your VIP benefits will continue until the end of the current paid subscription period. (3) Cancelling the subscription will not result in a refund for the current paid subscription period.
vipSubscriptionTerms.priceChanges: Price Changes: The platform reserves the right to adjust VIP Subscription pricing. Price changes will be notified to users in advance (via in-app notification or email). Price changes will only take effect upon renewal; if you do not accept the new price, you may cancel the subscription before renewal.
vipSubscriptionTerms.benefits: VIP Benefits: Benefits available to VIP members include but are not limited to: priority task listing, exclusive customer service channels, unlocking specific features, etc. Specific benefit content is subject to platform announcements within the app; the platform reserves the right to adjust VIP benefit content based on operational needs, and changes will be communicated to users via announcements or notifications.
vipSubscriptionTerms.limitations: Benefit Limitations: VIP benefits are for the personal use of the subscribing user only and may not be transferred, gifted, or shared. VIP status does not affect the user's obligation to comply with other terms of this Agreement. VIP benefits do not include any guarantee regarding the outcome of any task or transaction on the platform. The platform may set different benefits for different VIP tiers; specifics are subject to platform announcements.
vipSubscriptionTerms.refund: Refund Policy: Refunds for VIP Subscription are subject to the Apple App Store refund policy. To request a refund, please contact Apple Support or apply through Apple's official channels. The platform cannot directly process refunds for VIP Subscriptions purchased through the App Store.
vipSubscriptionTerms.trialPeriod: Free Trial (if applicable): The platform may from time to time offer free VIP trials. After the trial period ends, if the subscription is not cancelled, it will automatically convert to a paid subscription and be charged at the published price. Each user may only enjoy one free trial.
vipSubscriptionTerms.termination: Termination: If a user violates this Agreement or platform rules, the platform reserves the right to terminate their VIP Subscription without refund. After VIP Subscription termination, the user's VIP benefits will cease immediately.

accountDeletion.title: Account Deletion Policy
accountDeletion.intro: Under UK GDPR and related regulations, you have the right to request deletion of your account and personal data. These terms explain the process, conditions, and consequences of account deletion.
accountDeletion.process: Deletion Process: (1) You may request account deletion through the in-app 'Settings → Delete Account' function or by sending an email to info@link2ur.com. (2) After submitting a deletion request, the platform will process your request within 30 days (additional time may be needed for identity verification). (3) During this period, you may withdraw your deletion request at any time. (4) Once deletion processing is complete, you will receive a confirmation notification.
accountDeletion.conditions: Deletion Conditions: Account deletion may be temporarily delayed or partially restricted in the following circumstances: (1) You have tasks in progress (paid but not completed). (2) You have outstanding refund requests or disputes. (3) Your account has unsettled receivable/payable amounts. (4) Your account is under platform investigation or involved in legal proceedings. Once these matters are resolved, the deletion process may continue.
accountDeletion.consequences: Consequences of Deletion: After account deletion: (1) You will be unable to log in or access the account. (2) Tasks, reviews, forum posts, and other public content you posted may be anonymised or deleted. (3) Your points, coupons, VIP benefits, etc. will be permanently invalidated and cannot be recovered. (4) Your personal data will be handled in accordance with the Privacy Notice's data retention policy (see 'Data Retention' below). (5) Completed transaction records will be retained for the legally required period before deletion.
accountDeletion.dataRetention: Data Retention: Even after account deletion, the following data may continue to be retained as required by law: (1) Transaction and payment records: Up to 6 years under UK tax and accounting regulations. (2) Dispute resolution records: Retained for a reasonable period after the dispute is fully resolved. (3) Security logs: Retained for the minimum necessary period based on risk level. (4) Anonymised statistical data: May be permanently retained for platform operational analysis, but cannot be linked to you personally. Other personal data will be deleted or anonymised within 30 days after deletion processing is complete.
accountDeletion.reRegistration: Re-registration: After account deletion, you may register a new account using the same email, but data, points, review records, etc. from the original account cannot be recovered.

studentVerificationTerms.title: Student Verification Terms
studentVerificationTerms.intro: Link²Ur provides a student identity verification feature to verify users' student status and grant student-specific badges and related benefits. By using the student verification feature, you agree to these terms.
studentVerificationTerms.verificationProcess: Verification Process: (1) Users must provide a valid school/university educational email (.ac.uk or other recognised educational domain email) for verification. (2) The platform will send a verification link or code to that email; completing verification grants student-verified status. (3) The platform may request additional supporting materials (such as student ID photos) to confirm the authenticity of student identity.
studentVerificationTerms.dataCollection: Data Collection and Use: To complete student verification, the platform collects the following information: (1) School/university educational email address. (2) Associated school/university name. (3) Verification time and status. This information is used solely for verifying student identity, providing student-specific features (such as school-based forum categories), and platform operational analysis. See the relevant provisions in the Privacy Notice.
studentVerificationTerms.obligations: User Obligations: (1) Users must ensure that submitted student identity information is true and accurate. (2) Users must not use another person's educational email or forge student identity for verification. (3) If a user's student status changes (e.g. graduation, withdrawal), they should promptly update their verification status.
studentVerificationTerms.falseVerification: Consequences of False Verification: If a user is found to have provided false student identity information: (1) The platform will immediately revoke the student verification badge. (2) The platform may restrict or terminate the user's account. (3) Any benefits or privileges obtained through false student identity will be reclaimed. (4) The platform reserves the right to pursue legal action.
studentVerificationTerms.dataRetention: Data Retention: Student verification data is retained for the duration of verification validity. After verification expires or is revoked, related verification records will be handled according to the platform's data retention policy. When a user deletes their account, student verification data will be deleted accordingly (except where retention is legally required).
studentVerificationTerms.validity: Verification Validity: Student verification may have an expiry period (specific terms subject to platform announcements). After expiry, users may need to re-verify to maintain student-verified status.

termsOfService.importantNotice: Important Notice: Please read these terms carefully, especially the terms that exclude or limit liability. If you do not agree to any terms, please stop using the platform service.

privacyPolicy.title: Privacy Notice
privacyPolicy.dpoNote: (For data protection contact/DPO, please use the above email)

dataCollection.title: What Data We Collect & Processing Purposes (Legal Basis)
dataCollection.accountData: Account Data: Email, name (if provided), login records, etc. — For account creation and management, communication with you (contract performance/legitimate interests).
dataCollection.taskData: Task, Flea Market, Forum and Communication Data: Task and flea market listing content, timestamps, in-platform messages, forum posts and replies, and related communication records — For matching, listings, risk control and support (contract performance/legitimate interests).
dataCollection.locationData: Location Information: When you use the map location picker or allow the app to access location, we collect coordinates (latitude/longitude). We display only obfuscated locations (e.g. city or area). Location is used for: (1) selecting task/item locations, distance and matching; (2) location-based recommendations; (3) optimising recommendation algorithms. You may enter locations manually; location is not mandatory. GPS data is used for recommendation only and not shared with third parties (contract performance/legitimate interests).
dataCollection.recommendationData: Recommendation System Data: We collect browsing and search behaviour, interaction data (tasks/items you view, accept, skip or favourite), preference and historical data, as well as user profile data derived from AI assistant conversation analysis (interests, skills, predicted needs, etc.), to provide personalised task and content recommendations. You can opt out of personalised recommendations in settings (legitimate interests).
dataCollection.technicalData: Technical and Log Data: IP, device information, app version (web and iOS/Android), error logs, necessary Cookies — For security, debugging and abuse prevention (legitimate interests).
dataCollection.analyticsData: Optional Analytics Cookies (if enabled): For understanding usage and improving experience (consent).
dataCollection.paymentData: Payment Related: We process payments via Stripe (card, Apple Pay, WeChat Pay where available). We collect payment amount, status, time and relevant IDs for reconciliation and support. Full payment credentials are processed by Stripe/Apple/WeChat; we do not store them. VIP subscription purchases via the Apple App Store are subject to Apple’s terms and privacy policy; we receive only subscription status for access control (contract performance/legitimate interests).
dataCollection.aiChatData: AI Assistant Data: When you use the platform AI assistant 'Linker', we collect your conversation content, query records, and interaction data. Additionally, the AI assistant automatically analyses conversation content to extract your interest preferences (such as task categories of interest), skills, life stage signals (such as moving, graduation, etc.), and service preferences to build a user profile. This data is used for: (1) Providing and improving the AI assistant service; (2) Optimising response quality and personalised recommendations; (3) Powering the platform's task recommendation algorithm to match you with more relevant tasks and services; (4) Ensuring service security. User profile data is stored in aggregated form and does not contain original conversation text. AI conversation data is typically retained for no more than 12 months; user profile data is retained for the duration of your account and deleted upon account cancellation. You can opt out of AI-analysis-based personalised recommendations in settings. We do not use AI conversation or profile data for marketing or share it with third parties (contract performance/legitimate interests).
dataCollection.studentVerificationData: Student Verification Data: When you use the student verification feature, we collect your educational email address and associated school/university name. This data is used solely for verifying student identity, providing school-based community forum features, and platform operational analysis. Student verification data is retained for the duration of verification validity and deleted when the account is deleted (contract performance/legitimate interests).

dataSharing.title: Who We Share With
dataSharing.cloudServices: Cloud Services/Hosting and Security Vendors: Only shared to the extent necessary for service provision, with data processing agreements in place.
dataSharing.paymentProcessors: Payment and Subscription Processors (e.g. Stripe, Apple, WeChat Pay): Only to the extent necessary to process payments and VIP subscriptions; each has its own privacy policy.
dataSharing.legalDisclosure: Law Enforcement/Regulatory and Dispute Resolution: Disclose necessary information when legally required or to protect legitimate rights.

internationalTransfer.title: International Transfers
internationalTransfer.content: If personal data needs to be transferred outside the UK, we will use applicable transfer safeguards (such as UK IDTA or UK addendum to EU SCCs, etc.).

retentionPeriod.title: Retention Periods
retentionPeriod.accountData: Account and Basic Operational Data: Account duration + reasonable backup period.
retentionPeriod.transactionData: Transaction/Task and Customer Service Records: For accounting, tax or compliance purposes, typically retained for no more than 6 years (unless law requires longer/shorter).
retentionPeriod.recommendationData: Recommendation System Data: User browsing behavior, search records, interaction data and other data used for recommendations are typically retained for no more than 2 years, or until you delete your account. GPS location data is only used temporarily and not stored long-term.
retentionPeriod.securityLogs: Security Logs: Determined based on risk classification, kept for the shortest necessary period.
retentionPeriod.aiChatData: AI Assistant Conversation Data: Typically retained for no more than 12 months, or until the user actively deletes conversation records.
retentionPeriod.studentVerificationData: Student Verification Data: Retained for the duration of verification validity; handled according to platform data retention policy after verification expires or account is deleted.

yourRights.title: Your Rights
yourRights.content: You have the right to request access, rectification, erasure, restriction of processing, data portability, objection to processing, and to withdraw consent when based on consent (withdrawal does not affect the legality of processing before withdrawal).
yourRights.accountDeletion: Account Deletion and Data Erasure: You have the right to request deletion of your account and personal data at any time. You may submit a request via the in-app 'Settings → Delete Account' function or by emailing info@link2ur.com. We will process your request within 30 days. After deletion, your personal data will be deleted or anonymised, except for data that must be retained by law (e.g. transaction records retained for up to 6 years). See 'Account Deletion Policy' in the Terms of Service for details.
yourRights.complaintProcess: If you have questions or complaints about our data processing, please first contact info@link2ur.com by email; if you still have concerns, you may complain to the UK Information Commissioner's Office (ICO).

cookies.title: Cookies
cookies.necessary: Necessary Cookies: Essential for site operation.
cookies.optional: Analytics/Functional Cookies (Optional): Only enabled after your explicit consent; you can change preferences at any time in the Cookie banner or settings.

contactUs.title: Contact Us
contactUs.content: For this privacy notice or personal data matters, contact us anytime: info@link2ur.com

privacyPolicy.importantNotice: Important Notice: This privacy notice is an agreement between you and Link²Ur regarding personal information processing. We are committed to protecting your privacy rights in accordance with this notice.

cookiePolicy.title: Cookie Policy

intro.title: Introduction
intro.p1: This Cookie Policy explains how Link²Ur ("we", "our") uses cookies and similar technologies, and how you can manage these technologies. This policy supplements the Privacy Notice and should be read together with the Privacy Notice.
intro.p2: Under the UK Privacy and Electronic Communications Regulations (PECR) and the General Data Protection Regulation (UK GDPR), we must inform you about the cookies we use when you visit our website and obtain your consent for non-essential cookies.

whatAreCookies.title: 1. What are Cookies and Similar Technologies
whatAreCookies.p1: Cookies are small text files that are stored on your device (computer, tablet, or mobile device) when you visit a website. Cookies contain information that is transmitted back to the website when you return to the website or visit other websites that use the same cookies.
whatAreCookies.p2: In addition to cookies, we may also use other similar technologies, including:
whatAreCookies.p3: • Local Storage: Stores data in your browser, similar to cookies but can store more information.
whatAreCookies.p4: • Session Storage: Temporarily stores data, only valid during the browser session.
whatAreCookies.p5: • Pixel Tags: Small image files used to track webpage visits and email opens.
whatAreCookies.p6: • Device Identifiers: Unique identifiers used in mobile applications to identify devices.

typesWeUse.title: 2. Types of Cookies We Use
typesWeUse.p1: We categorize cookies based on their purpose and necessity as follows:
typesWeUse.p3: These cookies are essential for the basic functions of the website. Without these cookies, the website cannot function properly. These cookies do not require your consent. Including:
typesWeUse.p4: • Authentication Cookies: Used to maintain your login status and ensure you are authenticated.
typesWeUse.p5: • Security Cookies: Used to prevent fraud, abuse, and unauthorized access, protecting your account security.
typesWeUse.p6: • Load Balancing Cookies: Used to distribute traffic among multiple servers to ensure stable website operation.
typesWeUse.p7: • Shopping Cart/Session Cookies: Used to remember your actions on the website, such as items added to cart or form filling progress.
typesWeUse.p9: These cookies allow the website to remember your choices (such as language preferences, regional settings) to provide enhanced personalized features. These cookies are only enabled after your explicit consent. Including:
typesWeUse.p10: • Preference Cookies: Remember your language, theme, font size, and other settings.
typesWeUse.p11: • Functional Cookies: Remember your search history, favorite content, recently viewed items, etc.
typesWeUse.p12: • Geolocation Cookies: Remember your location preferences for providing localized content and services.
typesWeUse.p14: These cookies help us understand how users use the website so we can improve website performance and user experience. These cookies are only enabled after your explicit consent. Including:
typesWeUse.p15: • Website Analytics Cookies: Used for statistics on visits, page views, user dwell time, bounce rate, and other metrics.
typesWeUse.p16: • Performance Monitoring Cookies: Used to monitor website performance, error rates, loading times, etc., helping us optimize website speed.
typesWeUse.p17: • User Behavior Analysis Cookies: Used to analyze user behavior patterns on the website to understand user preferences and needs.
typesWeUse.p18: We may use third-party analytics services (such as Google Analytics), and these service providers have their own privacy policies. We recommend that you review these third parties' privacy policies to understand how they use cookies.
typesWeUse.p20: We currently do not use advertising cookies on our website. If we use advertising cookies in the future, we will update relevant information in this policy and obtain your explicit consent before use.

thirdParty.title: 3. Third-Party Cookies
thirdParty.p1: We may allow third-party service providers to set cookies on our website. These third-party cookies are subject to third-party privacy policies, and we are not responsible for them.
thirdParty.p2: Third-party services we may use include:
thirdParty.p3: • Analytics Services: Such as Google Analytics, for website analytics and performance monitoring.
thirdParty.p4: • Cloud Service Providers: For hosting and data storage.
thirdParty.p5: • Security Services: For preventing fraud and abuse.
thirdParty.p6: We recommend that you review these third parties' privacy policies and cookie policies to understand how they use cookies and your data.

retention.title: 4. Cookie Retention Periods
retention.p1: Different types of cookies have different retention periods:
retention.p2: • Session Cookies: These cookies are temporary and only valid during your visit to the website. These cookies are automatically deleted when you close your browser.
retention.p3: • Persistent Cookies: These cookies remain on your device for a period of time, even after you close your browser. The retention period for persistent cookies is usually 30 days to 2 years, depending on the type and purpose of the cookie. We will provide specific retention period information for each cookie type on the cookie settings page.
retention.p4: • Third-Party Cookies: The retention period for third-party cookies is determined by third-party service providers, which we cannot control. Please review the relevant third parties' privacy policies for detailed information.

howToManage.title: 5. How to Manage Cookies
howToManage.p1: You can manage cookies in the following ways:
howToManage.p3: You can change your cookie preferences at any time through the cookie settings page on our website. You can enable or disable functional cookies and analytics cookies, but please note that disabling certain cookies may affect website functionality and your user experience.
howToManage.p5: Most browsers allow you to manage cookies. You can:
howToManage.p6: • View cookies stored on your device
howToManage.p7: • Delete cookies (including all cookies or cookies from specific websites)
howToManage.p8: • Block cookies (block all cookies or only third-party cookies)
howToManage.p9: • Receive notifications before cookies are set
howToManage.p10: Please note that disabling or deleting cookies may affect website functionality. Some features may not work properly, and you may need to re-enter information or reset preferences.
howToManage.p12: In mobile applications, you can manage cookie-like technologies (such as in-app identifiers, device IDs, etc.) through app settings. You can also manage certain identifiers by resetting advertising identifiers in device settings.

mobileTech.title: 6. Similar Technologies in Mobile Applications
mobileTech.p1: In mobile applications, we may use cookie-like technologies to achieve similar functions, including:
mobileTech.p2: • In-App Identifiers: Used to identify app installations and user sessions.
mobileTech.p3: • Device IDs: Used to identify devices and provide personalized services.
mobileTech.p4: • Advertising Identifiers: Used to provide relevant advertising (if applicable).
mobileTech.p5: The use of these technologies follows the provisions of our Privacy Notice. You can manage these technologies through app settings or device settings.

yourRights.title: 7. Your Rights
yourRights.p1: Under UK GDPR and PECR, you have the following rights regarding cookies:
yourRights.p2: • Right to Information: You have the right to know which cookies we use and why we use them.
yourRights.p3: • Right to Consent: For non-essential cookies, you have the right to choose whether to consent to their use.
yourRights.p4: • Right to Withdraw Consent: You can withdraw your consent to non-essential cookies at any time.
yourRights.p5: • Right to Deletion: You can delete cookies stored on your device at any time.
yourRights.p6: • Right of Access: You can request access to your personal data collected through cookies.
yourRights.p7: If you wish to exercise these rights, please contact us at info@link2ur.com.

contactUs.title: 8. Contact Us
contactUs.p1: If you have any questions about this Cookie Policy or need help managing your cookie preferences, please contact us:
contactUs.p4: We will respond to your inquiries within a reasonable time.

importantNotice.title: Important Notice
importantNotice.p1: This Cookie Policy may be updated from time to time. We will notify you when the policy is updated and publish the updated version on this page. We recommend that you review this policy regularly to stay informed.

cookiePolicy.necessary: Necessary Cookies: Essential for basic site operation (e.g. login, security) and cannot be disabled.
cookiePolicy.optional: Analytics/Functional Cookies (Optional): Only enabled after your explicit consent; you can change preferences at any time in the Cookie banner or settings.

intro.title: Overview
